hero.title: Find the right therapist
hero.subtitle: A calmer way to find someone who fits, before the first appointment turns into guesswork.

howItWorks.label: How it works
howItWorks.title: Three clear steps
howItWorks.subtitle: A short path from uncertainty to a first conversation that actually feels considered.

steps.title: Share what feels most important
steps.text: A few questions help us understand your pace, preferences, and what kind of support would feel right.

steps.title: See three strong matches
steps.text: We narrow the field and explain why each person stands out, so you are not scanning a directory alone.

steps.title: Reach out when one feels right
steps.text: Watch a short intro, send a first message, or book a quick call from the recommendation itself.

comparison.label: Why Myötä
comparison.title: Less searching, more direction
directoryPoints: Hundreds of profiles, but no clear place to begin
No explanation for why a certain therapist might fit you
The first appointment can feel like a costly guess
Momentum gets lost before you even start

myotaPoints.text: A short match flow narrows the field quickly
Every recommendation comes with a reason:
You can watch an intro or reach out directly from the result
The first session feels chosen, not random

testimonials.label: Testimonials
testimonials.title: What people say after they finally start
testimonials.subtitle: A few voices from people who moved from browsing to a more considered first step.

items.quote: I expected another long directory and more uncertainty. Instead, I got three thoughtful recommendations and understood why each one could work for me.
items.name: Emilia, 31

items.quote: The deeper version helped me notice what kind of therapist I actually needed, not just what topic I wanted to talk about. That changed everything.
items.name: Jonas, 42

items.quote: What I appreciated most was that the first step felt gentle. I did not need perfect words, and I still felt understood.
items.name: Mira, 27

faq.label: FAQs
faq.title: Questions that usually come up
faq.subtitle: The practical details, without repeating the whole pitch.
faq.ctaText: Start with the standard match flow, or choose the longer version if you want to give the recommendation more detail.

items.question: How long does this take?
items.answer: Most people can finish the main flow in about two minutes. There is also a longer version if you want to give the match more context.

items.question: Do I need to know exactly what is wrong before I start?
items.answer: No. Many people begin when they can only describe a feeling, not a clear label. The questions are designed to work with that uncertainty rather than against it.

items.question: Will I only see one recommendation?
items.answer: No. You receive three recommendations, along with a short explanation of why each one could be a good fit for you.

items.question: Can I start with the short quiz and go deeper later?
items.answer: Yes. You can begin with the shorter path and switch to the longer version later if you want a fuller recommendation.

footer.quote: "The first therapy appointment should feel safe, not like a guess."

short.title: A short match, then a clearer next step.
short.description: This version keeps things light and gets you to a thoughtful recommendation quickly.
short.switchDescription: The longer path adds more detail about pace, preferences, and how you want the work to feel.

long.title: A fuller picture for a more precise match.
long.description: This version asks a little more so the recommendation can be more specific.
long.switchCta: Go back to the short version
long.switchDescription: You can always return to the shorter version if you want to move faster.

short.title: Short introduction
short.description: 7 questions, about 2 minutes. A lighter first step when you simply want to get moving.

long.title: Deeper introduction
long.description: 16 questions, about 5 minutes. More nuance, more detail, and a softer path toward a more precise match.

ui.gentleReminderText: You do not need perfect words to continue. A good match often begins with a few honest signals, not a complete explanation.
ui.reminderShort: You can always add more detail later.
ui.reminderLong: You can always switch back to the shorter path.
ui.multiSelectHint: Choose more than one if needed
ui.textPlaceholder: Write as much or as little as you want.
ui.recommendationsSubtitle: These are not random names from a directory. Each recommendation is tied to the kind of support, pace, and first impression you told us you were hoping for.
ui.bookCallCopy: A short first call can help you hear their tone, ask about availability, and decide whether the first session feels right.
ui.ctaTitle: A good first step should feel more grounded than random.
ui.ctaText: You can take the quiz again, compare your recommendations once more, or head back to the homepage and continue from there when it feels right.

questions.s1: What best describes what feels heaviest for you right now?
questions.s2: What language would you like to use with the professional?
questions.s3: What kind of support feels most important right now?
questions.s4: Does the therapist's gender matter to you?
questions.s5: Does the therapist's age matter to you?
questions.s6: What price range feels realistic to you per session?
questions.s6_note: These ranges are indicative and may vary by professional.
questions.s7: Would you like to use Kela reimbursement if possible?
questions.s7_note: Kela may reimburse part of psychotherapy costs under certain conditions in Finland.
questions.l1: What best describes what feels heaviest for you right now?
questions.l2: What language would you like to use with the professional?
questions.l3: How long has this been weighing on you?
questions.l4: What area of life would you most want to talk about? You can choose more than one.
questions.l5: How disruptive does this feel in your everyday life right now?
questions.l5_note: If you feel in crisis, the fastest available support is always the most important next step.
questions.l6: How do you usually work through difficult things?
questions.l7: What kind of professional would feel most natural to you?
questions.l8: What pace would feel right for you?
questions.l9: Would you prefer to meet remotely or in person?
questions.l10: What session frequency sounds right to you?
questions.l11: What price range feels realistic to you per session?
questions.l12: Would you like to use Kela reimbursement if possible?
questions.l12_note: Kela may reimburse part of psychotherapy costs under certain conditions in Finland.
questions.l13: Have you been in therapy or received similar professional support before?
questions.l14: If you have been before, what felt good or what felt missing?
questions.l15: How would you describe your relationship to your own emotions?
questions.l16: Is there anything else you would want a therapist to know before a first appointment?
questions.l16_note: Write as little or as much as you want. This helps add nuance to the match.

answers.s1a: Daily life or work feels heavy and my energy is under strain
answers.s1b: There is one specific thing on my mind that I want to talk about
answers.s1c: A big life change is happening and I need support around it
answers.s1d: I cannot quite name it yet, it just feels unclear and heavy
answers.s3a: A safe space to unpack what is going on without pressure
answers.s3b: Concrete tools I can try in everyday life
answers.s3c: Help understanding why I react the way I do
answers.s3d: A clearer direction, I want movement and change
answers.s6d: Price is not the deciding factor for me
answers.s7a: Yes, show only therapists who fit that path
answers.s7b: It is helpful, but not essential
answers.s7c: I am not sure yet, tell me more later
answers.l1a: Daily life or work feels heavy and my energy is under strain
answers.l1b: There is one specific thing on my mind that I want to talk about
answers.l1c: A big life change is happening and I need support around it
answers.l1d: I cannot quite name it yet, it just feels unclear and heavy
answers.l3a: Quite recently, it still feels new
answers.l3c: For a longer time, more than a year
answers.l3d: For as long as I can remember, it has always been there in some way
answers.l4e: Something else, I can explain it in my own words later
answers.l5a: I am coping, but I wanted to seek support early
answers.l5b: Daily life still works somehow, but my energy is running low
answers.l5c: It feels quite heavy and I need help now
answers.l6a: I talk to people close to me, connection helps
answers.l6b: I think about things quietly on my own
answers.l6c: I do something concrete, action helps
answers.l7a: Warm and empathetic, I mainly want to feel heard
answers.l7b: Knowledgeable and structured, I want to understand what is happening
answers.l7c: Direct, I can appreciate challenging observations too
answers.l7d: I am not sure yet, I would rather discover it as I go
answers.l8a: Slowly and at my own pace, no rush
answers.l8b: Goal-oriented, I want to feel progress
answers.l8c: Either can work, I am flexible
answers.l9a: Remote, video sessions would work well for me
answers.l9b: In person, physical presence matters to me
answers.l10a: Once a week, I want to move actively
answers.l10b: A couple of times a month
answers.l10d: I am not sure yet, I would rather try first
answers.l11d: Price is not the deciding factor for me
answers.l12b: It is helpful, but not essential
answers.l12c: I am not sure yet, I would like to hear more
answers.l13a: No, this would be my first experience
answers.l13c: Yes, but something about it did not feel right
answers.l13d: I am currently in therapy and looking for something alongside it or in place of it
answers.l14a: I felt heard, but concrete tools were missing
answers.l14b: There was a lot of theory, but the human connection felt distant
answers.l14c: It worked well, I am looking for something similar
answers.l14d: I do not have earlier experience, I will skip this
answers.l15a: They feel very present, sometimes almost too much
answers.l15b: They feel distant, it is hard to tell what I feel
answers.l15c: I can function with them, but something still feels stuck
answers.l15d: I have not really thought about that before
answers.l16_placeholder: Write as much or as little as you want.

Anna Mäkinen.preview: A calm first meeting focused on practical relief and emotional clarity.
Anna Mäkinen.videoIntro: Anna opens with a grounded introduction to how she works: warm, clear, and gently practical. Her short intro is a good fit if you want to feel understood quickly without losing structure.
videoPoints: How she helps people who feel overloaded by daily life
What a first session looks like in practice
Why clarity and warmth can coexist
Anna Mäkinen.contactCopy: If Anna feels like the right first direction, the next step is simply a brief message. You do not need to explain everything yet.
Anna Mäkinen.title: Cognitive psychotherapist

Juhani Leppänen.preview: A slower, more reflective introduction for people who want more depth.
Juhani Leppänen.videoIntro: Juhani's introduction is thoughtful and unhurried. He speaks about long-form work, reflective conversations, and how therapy can help when something deeper has been building for a while.
videoPoints: What deeper therapeutic work can feel like at the beginning
How he creates room for reflection without pressure
Why longer patterns sometimes need a slower rhythm
Juhani Leppänen.contactCopy: A first message to Juhani can be short and simple. It is enough to say what has been feeling heavy and that you are looking for an opening.
Juhani Leppänen.title: Psychodynamic therapist

Sari Rantanen.preview: A gentle, practical style with tools you can carry into daily life.
Sari Rantanen.videoIntro: Sari's intro feels light, reassuring, and practical. She explains how she helps people begin with a low threshold while still building movement and confidence over time.
videoPoints: How practical tools are introduced without overwhelm
Why remote appointments can still feel personal
What a low-pressure first step can look like
Sari Rantanen.contactCopy: If Sari seems like a strong fit, you can begin with a short note or book a brief call to see how the first session might feel.
Sari Rantanen.title: ACT therapist
Sari Rantanen.availability: Remote appointments also available in the evenings

language.fallback: in the language that feels natural to you

supportNeed.s3a: a safe and pressure-free space to unpack what is going on
supportNeed.s3b: practical tools that can help in everyday life
supportNeed.s3c: help understanding the roots of your reactions
supportNeed.l7b: a structured and thoughtful working style
supportNeed.l7d: space to discover the right fit without forcing it
supportNeed.fallback: a way of being met that does not ask too much of you at once

pace.l8a: slowly and at your own pace
pace.l8b: with clear goals and a steady rhythm
pace.l8c: with flexibility depending on the moment
pace.fallback: at a pace that feels right for you

focus.s1a: carrying strain around energy, work, or everyday life
focus.s1b: holding one particular issue heavily in your mind
focus.s1c: moving through a major life change
focus.s1d: trying to name something that still feels vague
focus.l1a: carrying strain around energy, work, or everyday life
focus.l1b: holding one particular issue heavily in your mind
focus.l1c: moving through a major life change
focus.l1d: trying to name something that still feels vague
focus.fallback: whatever in life feels most alive for you right now

reasons.anna: Anna rises first because you described needing {{supportNeed}}. Her way of working is clear, warm, and practical, which often feels especially supportive when you are {{focus}}. She can meet {{sessionMode}} and shape a first appointment {{pace}}.
reasons.juhani: Juhani is a strong alternative when you want to pause more deeply and understand how the current strain has grown over time. He can be a particularly good fit when there are layers beneath the surface, but you still want a calm rhythm and room to think aloud {{language}}.
reasons.sari: Sari is a good third recommendation if you hope therapy will leave you with practical tools to carry into daily life. Her approach often helps when you want both compassion and a little concrete forward movement. This option works especially well if you want to begin {{sessionMode}} and keep the threshold as gentle as possible.
